title: dactylorhiza comosa subsp. scotica
intro: Dactylorhiza comosa subsp. scotica, also known as the Scottish Summer Orchid, is a species of orchid native to Scotland. It is an orchid species that belongs to the Dactylorhiza genus, which is often used in traditional herbal medicine. The medicinal properties of Dactylorhiza comosa subsp. scotica include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant has been used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its herbal preparations include tinctures, infusions, and dried root extracts that can be used in the treatment of these conditions.
